description_long: This sculpture depicts a draped human figure standing upright on a rectangular plinth. The figure is missing its head and arms, with the right arm partially preserved and bent at the elbow, holding the drapery. The garment is rendered with detailed, flowing folds that wrap around the body, emphasizing the contours and weight of the fabric. The back of the figure shows the drapery cascading in naturalistic folds, while the front reveals a more tightly wrapped form, suggesting a classical or Greco-Roman style. The surface appears to be made of stone, with a matte, slightly weathered finish. The base is simple and unadorned, serving to elevate and stabilize the sculpture.
description: A headless, draped human figure in stone, standing upright on a rectangular plinth with detailed garment folds and a classical style.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the back of the draped figure, with cascading folds and the right arm bent behind the body.
description_view_1: Right side view highlighting the profile of the figure, the bent right arm, and the depth of the drapery folds.
description_view_2: Back-left view emphasizing the layered garment and the curvature of the figure's stance.
description_view_3: Front-left view showing the tightly wrapped drapery over the torso and the absence of the head and arms.
attribute_location_context: No specific setting is visible; the sculpture is isolated on a plain background, suggesting a studio or catalog environment.
